meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Debugging with AI to Solve No SaaS Knowledge
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with code errors. This beginner-friendly checklist helps freelancers debug SaaS apps faster using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: You don't need a CS degree to ship SaaS. By leveraging AI-driven debugging tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can isolate stack traces, refactor broken logic, and implement features in minutes. Treat your AI as a senior engineering partner to turn technical blockers into completed client deliverables.
content.intro:
The blank screen is a freelancer's greatest enemy. You have the vision, the client's deposit, and the deadline, but the code refuses to compile. The 'no SaaS knowledge' wall feels like an impenetrable barrier between you and your next paycheck. Most developers spend hours hunting for missing semicolons; you shouldn't have to.

Today, we shift the paradigm. We stop treating code as a monolith and start treating it as a conversation. By integrating Antigravity for rapid architectural analysis and Claude Code for surgical refactoring, you can bypass the traditional learning curve. This isn't just about fixing bugs; it's about building a scalable development workflow that makes you look like a veteran engineer.

steps.title: Isolate the Error with Claude Code
steps.description: Before fixing, identify the exact failure point. Claude Code excels at parsing logs that would take a human hours to decipher.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the provided error logs and identify the root cause within the file structure. Explain the failure in plain English for a non-technical stakeholder.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'analyze the error logs in /logs and identify the root cause'

steps.title: Map Dependencies with Antigravity
steps.description: Visualize how your code components interact to ensure a fix in one area doesn't break a feature in another.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a dependency map for the current codebase and highlight any circular dependencies or deprecated modules.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'map the dependency graph and identify potential breaking points'

steps.title: Refactor Broken Logic
steps.description: Use AI to rewrite inefficient or buggy functions into clean, production-ready code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to be more efficient, adding error handling and logging for production monitoring.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor the file src/utils/auth.ts to handle edge cases'

steps.title: Automated Unit Testing
steps.description: Prevent regressions by generating tests for every piece of code you fix.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a suite of unit tests for the refactored code using Jest, covering both success and failure scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate unit tests for the modified modules and run them'

steps.title: Final Deployment Validation
steps.description: Perform a final check to ensure the production environment mirrors your local success.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current configuration for deployment readiness. Check for environment variable leaks or missing dependencies.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'verify production configuration and check for security leaks'

pro_tips: Always ask the AI to explain 'why' a fix works to build your own long-term technical intuition.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to keep context of previous project constraints across different sessions.
When stuck, ask the AI to 'act as a Senior Staff Engineer' to force higher-quality, architectural-level solutions.
Keep your prompt history organized in a Notion doc; you will inevitably encounter the same bugs in future projects.
Prioritize 'fail-safe' code generation—ask the AI to include comprehensive error handling by default.
content.next_steps: Now that you have debugged your first module, deploy it to a staging environment and run a smoke test. Once confirmed, document the solution in your personal knowledge base to speed up future client builds.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting the code and start shipping features that scale. With these tools, you are no longer just a freelancer—you are a high-velocity AI-augmented builder. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
